body.title: CLOSED: Make Discord go — bidirectional, 20-message no-drop acceptance
body.result: Discord reply delivery wired: main.py delivers LLM responses back to originating Discord channel_id; emit_channels.DiscordChannel stub replaced with discord_bot.send() + basket channel_id injection. Also swept 10 more files for ? placeholders (continuation of 0236d93a). 4479 tests pass. Igor needs restart to pick up changes.
body.description:
Discord is half-working: inbound messages arrive (igor0001#7027 connects, receives), but outbound is broken in two ways: (1) emit_channels.DiscordChannel is an explicit stub (emit_channels.py:177-183, drops with warning 'not yet implemented'), (2) main.py:1466 calls discord_bot.send() directly, bypassing the UC DiscordTransport that T-uc-channel-migration built.

Acceptance criteria: send 20 messages in each direction without a drop. Both paths must be wired — Igor sends via DiscordTransport/comms:// routing, inbound events flow through the normal input path.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/emit_channels.py (DiscordChannel stub), wild_igor/igor/main.py:1466 (direct discord_bot.send call), wild_igor/igor/network/discord_bot.py, agent_datacenter DiscordTransport
**Design rules:** comms routing through UC (T-uc-channel-migration design); no direct discord_bot.send() outside Transport wrapper
**Scope boundary:** wire existing DiscordTransport into outbound path; fix emit_channels stub; validate 20-msg no-drop round-trip. No new Discord features.
**Test plan:** manual 20-message send + receive in TheIgors Discord; confirm no 'not yet implemented' warnings; confirm outbound goes through DiscordTransport
